community.text: Cella is open-source and wants to work together with other OS projects & teams.
community.title: Faster together
call_to_action.intro: Interested in building your new app with Cella? Let's work together!
cards.blocknote.text: Block-based rich-text editor with real-time collaboration options and a minimal setup
cards.brevo.text: Brevo sends transactional emails and more.
cards.gleap.text: Gleap is an all-in-one customer feedback tool. Live chat, customer satisfaction, public roadmap and more.
cards.matrix.text: Matrix is an open network for secure, decentralized communication. Real-time chat, VoIP and more.
cards.onedollarstats.text: Affordable web analytics. Track event effortlessly. Access insights through dashboards or mobile app.
cards.paddle.text: Complete billing solution: Accept payments, handle taxes and manage subscriptions.
cards.scaleway.text: Scaleway offers compute, object storage, managed postgres, generative API.
cards.transloadit.text: Transloadit is a file handling service that helps you with uploading, transforming and storing files.
cella_approach: Cella has a selective sync engine in its template. It's a better fit if:
cella_approach_intro: Existing sync frameworks and sync databases can help you. But they require you to hand over control of your schemas, queries, and storage. It's either all-or-nothing, or you maintain two systems.
cella_approach_point_1: Only selected data models need sync
cella_approach_point_2: A REST API should stay authoritative
cella_approach_point_3: Lock-in or framework constraints should be avoided
compare_intro: Have a look at how Cella compares to existing sync solutions such as Electric, Zero, PowerSync and more ..
features.text: For a complete list of features, <featuresLink>click here</featuresLink>.
features.title: Core features
features.activity_bus.text: Every change from CDC becomes an event you can listen to anywhere in the API. It's used to send SSE notifications, but you can use it for other things too — like logging or webhooks.
features.at_least_once.text: The worker tracks its exact position in the database log and only acknowledges a change after it's processed. A crash or restart resumes from the last confirmed point, so nothing is silently dropped.
features.attachment.text: An optional synced example entity you can use today: resumable uploads, signed URLs and live updates per organisation.
features.autogenerated_docs.text: Your routes build an OpenAPI spec on each code change, and the live API docs render straight from it, always in step with your endpoints.
features.blocknote_editor.text: A block-based rich text editor with real-time collaboration and presence.
features.cache_integrity.text: On reconnect, the client compares its cached totals against the server's to catch any missed create or delete, at no extra cost.
features.cache_persistence.text: On the client, the query cache is saved to the browser so the app loads instantly and keeps working offline. Each save only writes the entries that actually changed, instead of rewriting the whole cache.
features.cache_token.text: Each SSE notification carries a signed token that authorises a stale client to read the server-side entity cache instead of hitting the database.
features.canonical_queries.text: The sync engine keeps a single list per entity up to date, and narrower or filtered views are derived from it on the client instead of fetching again.
features.catchup_recovery.text: After the CDC worker falls behind and replays the backlog of missed database changes, counters are recalculated from source data and entity caches are refreshed, so totals and badges correct themselves instead of drifting.
features.cdc_batching.text: Many row changes in one transaction collapse into a single live update per type, keeping traffic light.
features.cella_cli.text: Pull bug fixes and new features from upstream Cella into your fork with minimal merge friction.
features.circuit_breaker.text: One table's repeated failures can't stall the whole CDC processing pipeline. Its changes are skipped during a short cooldown, then automatically retested and resumed.
features.context_entities.text: Entities that have members and roles. Each user's membership and permissions are merged in the client, no extra API calls.
features.create_edit_coalescing.text: Create and edit offline, and one request sends the final result. Create then delete, and nothing is sent at all.
features.data_grid.text: A responsive data grid with sorting, cell selection, edit options and column management.
features.docker.text: Your Compose file is generated from one typed config, so service names never drift and type checks catch mistakes before deploy.
features.drizzle_orm.text: Type-safe, schema-first database access. Change a schema and generate the migration with one command.
features.email_templates.text: Type-safe transactional emails with translations. You can preview them in Storybook.
features.embedded_propagation.text: Nested data is tricky. Change a source entity once and every embedded copy of it is patched directly in the client's React Query cache wherever it appears.
features.entity_hierarchy.text: Define your entities (ie. users, organisations, projects) in a hierarchical config that drives security, permissions, and more automatically.
features.eu_cloud.text: EU-based hosting for data residency and compliance from day one.
features.file_based_routing.text: Drop a file in the routes folder and it's registered into a type-safe route tree automatically.
features.gap_detection.text: On reconnect, the client knows exactly what changed while you were away and fetches only that, including removals.
features.github_actions.text: Push to main and CI ships it.
features.guard_chain.text: Every request passes through layered checks for session, tenant and membership before any code runs, and writes are guarded again at the database by composite foreign keys and immutability triggers — no single layer is trusted alone.
features.i18n.text: Built-in translations with instant language switching. English and Dutch included.
features.idempotent_mutations.text: Every change has a stable id, so retrying after a dropped response or a crash never causes duplicates.
features.immutability.text: Key identity fields can't be changed after creation, and the activity log can only be added to, never edited.
features.impersonation.text: Admins can safely act as any user for support and debugging. Locked behind an IP allowlist.
features.load_testing.text: Repeatable load tests with saved baselines. Run them using a little CLI.
features.magic_link.text: Passwordless sign-in by default. A single-use link sent by email, rate-limited and brute-force protected.
features.mfa.text: Add a second factor with a passkey or authenticator app, and require re-confirmation for sensitive actions.
features.mock_generators.text: One source powers API examples, database seeds and load-test data. Always in step with your schemas.
features.multi_tab.text: One tab holds the live connection and shares updates with the rest. Every tab can edit, none connects twice.
features.notify_then_fetch.text: When data changes, you get a tiny live signal and the client fetches just the changed rows. No polling, no bloated payloads.
features.oauth.text: Let users sign in with GitHub, Google or Microsoft, secured with modern OAuth best practices.
features.offline_queue.text: Edits made offline are saved locally and replay automatically when you reconnect. With conflicts kept rare.
features.openapi_extensions.text: Guards, rate limits and caches are declared per endpoint and shown automatically in the docs.
features.openapi_sdk.text: Your routes build an OpenAPI spec on each code change, and a fully typed client SDK, with schemas, types and a fetch client, is generated straight from it.
features.organization.text: The built-in organisation entity with members, invitations and roles you can adjust to fit your app.
features.otel.text: End-to-end traces, metrics and logs across the whole stack with OpenTelemetry.
features.page.text: An optional public page entity with real-time co-editing.
features.passkey.text: Passkeys can be the primary or the secondary auth method. No passwords to leak or reset.
features.per_field_merge.text: Each field type resolves on its own, so edits never collide: simple fields (name, status) keep the most recent change, and lists (labels, assignees) merge everyone's additions and removals. No 409s, no lost work.
features.permission_manager.text: Role-based access that understands your hierarchy, resolving who can do what by walking memberships up the tree.
features.product_entities.text: Your everyday content models, served over normal REST endpoints with offline support and real-time sync built in.
features.public_private.text: Two live streams: a private one for your org data and a public one for shareable content. Each with its own scope.
features.pulumi_iac.text: An interactive CLI guides you through setup, preview, deploy and secrets, with Pulumi keeping the whole stack as repeatable infrastructure as code.
features.pwa.text: An installable app that works offline, with assets cached for true offline use.
features.rate_limiting.text: Login endpoints are rate-limited and brute-force protected, so credential stuffing hits a wall.
features.resizable_panels.text: Resizable, collapsible panels that remember their layout between sessions.
features.responsive.text: Mobile-first layouts that adapt cleanly from phone to desktop, with drawers and similar components giving a native feel on mobile.
features.rest_fallback.text: If the live stream drops, the client keeps working as a normal REST app, refetching on navigation and resuming live updates automatically once it reconnects.
features.rls.text: Tenant-scoped data tables are locked down in Postgres with row-level security: reads fail closed, so without the right tenant context a query simply returns nothing. Writes lean on the guard chain and database constraints instead, keeping RLS a cheap per-row tenant check rather than a costly membership lookup on the hot write path.
features.schema_evolution.text: Change your data model without breaking older clients. Migrations and adapters keep new and old client versions talking.
features.seen_unseen.text: Track who has seen each item and show live unseen counts. Perfect for notification badges.
features.session_management.text: Users can see where they're logged in and sign out any device they don't recognise.
features.soft_delete.text: Removed items are marked as deleted, not erased, so they stay in sequence and all clients can catch up on removals.
features.storybook.text: Build and review components in isolation with Storybook.
features.stream_reconnect.text: When the live SSE stream drops, the client reconnects with backoff and jitter, and a circuit breaker backs off a struggling server instead of hammering it.
features.tenant_isolation.text: A hard boundary in the database keeps each tenant's data fully separate. No accidental cross-tenant access.
features.text_merge.text: Long-form descriptions merge character by character through a Yjs CRDT, so two people typing in the same paragraph keep both sets of changes instead of overwriting each other.
features.totp.text: Time-based one-time codes as a second factor, available once a passkey is set up.
features.transient_retry.text: When the CDC worker hits a passing database hiccup — a timeout, deadlock or dropped connection — it retries with exponential backoff, while real errors fail fast instead of looping.
features.ttl_cache.text: On the server, a short-lived cache turns a burst of concurrent requests for the same entity into a single database query, shared by every waiting client.
features.ui_components.text: A large set of ready-made React components, most of them built on Base UI.
features.unified_counts.text: Live totals per context live in one place, updated by CDC on every change. They power UI counters and sync checks with no extra queries.
features.user.text: A ready-made user entity. Profile, settings, sessions, passkeys and social logins. Wired into auth out of the box.
features.vitest.text: Preconfigured Vitest across the monorepo, with shared config and database-backed tests ready to run.
features.yjs_collaboration.text: Real-time co-editing with live presence, powered by Yjs. While connected, concurrent edits merge cleanly with no conflicts.
features.zero_downtime.text: Each deploy boots a new version alongside the running one and only shifts traffic over after it passes a health and version check, keeping the old version as a rollback target.
hero.text: Cella is a TypeScript template to build web apps that need offline & real-time sync features. Postgres, OpenAPI & React Query are authorative.
hero.title: Ship modern web apps. Use a classic REST API.

pricing.plan_2.title: receive €1-12k
pricing.plan_3.title: To be decided
pricing.text_1: 50% to sponsor OS libraries and 50% to a Cella bounty program.
pricing.text_2: Earn while building your own product. Open-source or 'community-source' parts of your code.
pricing.text_3: Do you have a product that integrates well with CellaJS? Become a partner.

faq.title: FAQs
integrations.title: Integrations
pricing.text: How to build a dedicated & sustainable community around CellaJS? Membership perhaps.
pricing.title: Pricing (conceptual)
selective_sync.text: Many modern, collaborative web apps need a UI that is instant, a real-time sync to show live data and the ability to work while offline. But building this yourself is hard.
selective_sync.title: A selective sync engine
how.text: Cella's selective sync engine is built around two guiding principles:
how.concept_1: Sync processes don't compete, they complement the REST API
how.concept_2: Synced data should behave & be treated like non-synced data
how.title: Sync architecture
sync_diagram.part_1.label: Part 1
sync_diagram.part_1.text: A REST API (OpenAPI 3.1) is authoritative: all data eventually passes through it. Permissions are checked on every request. 3rd party services can use it. When the sync engine is down, clients still get fresh data.
sync_diagram.part_2.label: Part 2
sync_diagram.part_2.text: The sync engine has a notify-then-fetch pattern: The CDC (Change Data Capture) worker subscribes to a WAL (Write-Ahead Log), processes the logs, and sends changes back to the API → API sends an SSE to notify the client about a change → client then does a normal API request.
sync_diagram.part_3.label: Part 3
sync_diagram.part_3.text: For collaborative editing & online presence, the Yjs worker sends live changes to clients. Permission is verified directly against the DB, where it persists the merged document state. Those not editing, still receive an SSE notification every few seconds.
showcase.title: Build something real, launch faster
showcase.text: A showcase of real-world products built with Cella.
showcase.text_1: An issue tracker to manage multiple app projects from a single page view. Built for speed & fun.
showcase.from_cella: Of all code is from Cella
stack.artillery.text: Load testing for critical flows has been made easy.
stack.baseui.text: Accessible components with BaseUI, Tailwind and Motion.
stack.drizzle.text: Postgres DB with Drizzle ORM. Seeds and easy migrations.
stack.hono.text: A simple and fast node server, easily extended with middleware.
stack.openapi.text: Autogenerated types, zod schemas, client SDK & API docs.
stack.pulumi.text: Infrastructure as code for repeatable setup. Deploy using GitHub Actions.
stack.react.text: React with Tanstack Router. Tanstack Query for fetching and caching.
stack.text: Best-in-class libraries that play nice together. Not hidden behind a framework.
stack.title: A proven stack
stack.vite.text: A straightforward and fast monorepo setup. Vite PWA included.
stack.yjs.text: Real-time collaboration with presence as part of a rich-text editor.
why.text_2: Some data is <syncEngineLink>synced in real-time</syncEngineLink> to clients, some is just fetched. All data passes through Postgres, API & React Query.
why.text_1: Auth, permissions, a modern UI, automated SDK & docs, CLI to pull template changes, <featuresLink>and more</featuresLink>.
why.text_3: A Github Actions workflow and CLI allow you to deploy everything on European infrastructure.
